title: racosperma juliferum subsp. curvinervium
intro: Racosperma juliferum subsp. curvinervium, also known as the yellow cassia or Cassia auriculata, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Racosperma juliferum subsp. curvinervium include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
